subtitle: Cross-platform app development with live DartPad
project: Campus Coffee — a college coffee-shop app you build lesson by lesson

challenge.title: Build Your First Widget
challenge.description: Edit the loaded code so the app shows a GreetingCard: a title "Welcome to Campus Coffee", a short description underneath, and an ElevatedButton labelled "Get Started".
challenge.hint: Create a StatelessWidget called GreetingCard. Return a Column with two Text widgets and an ElevatedButton. Wrap it in Center so it sits in the middle of the screen.

challenge.title: Product Card
challenge.description: The loaded code shows a plain menu item. Upgrade it into a Card with the drink name in bold, a one-line description, the price in green, and an "Add" button in a Row.
challenge.hint: Wrap everything in Card > Padding > Column. Use crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, SizedBox for spacing, and a Row with mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.spaceBetween for the price and button.

challenge.title: Menu Grid
challenge.description: Convert the ListView menu in the loaded code into a two-column GridView.builder, each cell showing an icon above the drink name inside a Card.
challenge.hint: Use GridView.builder with SliverGridDelegateWithFixedCrossAxisCount(crossAxisCount: 2). Each itemBuilder returns a Card containing a Column with an Icon and a Text.

challenge.title: Basket Counter
challenge.description: The loaded app shows a basket count that never changes. Make it a StatefulWidget with + and − buttons that update the count via setState(), never dropping below zero.
challenge.hint: Convert OrderBasket to a StatefulWidget. Store `int count = 0;` in the State class, call setState(() => count++) in the button handlers, and guard the decrement with `if (count > 0)`.

challenge.title: Drink Details Screen
challenge.description: Tapping a menu item currently does nothing. Use Navigator.push to open a DetailScreen showing the tapped drink's name and price, with the AppBar back button returning to the menu.
challenge.hint: In the ListTile onTap, call Navigator.push(context, MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => DetailScreen(drink: drinks[index]))). Pass the drink through the DetailScreen constructor.

challenge.title: Brand the App
challenge.description: Give Campus Coffee a brand: set a ColorScheme.fromSeed theme with a coffee-brown seed colour, style the AppBar from the theme, and add a dark theme the user can toggle.
challenge.hint: In MaterialApp set theme: ThemeData(colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.brown)) and darkTheme with brightness: Brightness.dark. Hold a ThemeMode in state and flip it with an IconButton.

challenge.title: Loading the Menu
challenge.description: The menu currently appears instantly. Simulate a network call: load it with a Future that takes 2 seconds, show a CircularProgressIndicator while waiting, and render the list when it arrives using FutureBuilder.
challenge.hint: Write `Future<List<String>> fetchMenu() async { await Future.delayed(const Duration(seconds: 2)); return [...]; }` then wrap the body in FutureBuilder and branch on snapshot.connectionState and snapshot.hasData.

challenge.title: Fix the Broken Till
challenge.description: The loaded app has three deliberate bugs: the total miscalculates, one button does nothing, and the console shows an assertion in debug mode. Use debugPrint and the error messages to find and fix all three.
challenge.hint: Bug 1: look at the operator in _addToTotal. Bug 2: check which callback the second button is wired to (and whether it's null). Bug 3: read the assert message — the VAT rate constant breaks the assertion.
